headline: Russian-speaking attackers' FortiBleed haul reaches 86,644 Fortinet devices as a Klue integration breach spreads to security vendors and Microsoft warns AI browsing agents can be hijacked into remote code execution.

emerging_trends.text: Defenders now treat autonomous AI agents as privileged identities after Microsoft's AutoJack showed a lone webpage can ride an AI browsing agent into full host compromise.
emerging_trends.topic_title: AutoJack Turns AI Browsing Agents Into RCE

emerging_trends.text: OAuth-token and integration-credential abuse keeps turning trusted SaaS connectors into breach vectors, with the Klue compromise reaching even security vendors Huntress and Recorded Future.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Klue Breach Cascades Into Salesforce Theft

emerging_trends.text: Critical unauthenticated remote code execution keeps landing in the internet's plumbing, from FortiGate gateways to F5's NGINX web server.
emerging_trends.topic_title: F5 Patches Critical NGINX Open Source RCE Flaws

topics.title: Klue Breach Cascades Into Salesforce Theft
topics.latest_developments: Salesforce disabled the Klue Battlecards integration over the June 11 compromise, and Recorded Future joined Huntress among confirmed victims as Klue became the third connected app abused to siphon Salesforce data.
topics.summary: A single compromised Klue integration credential cascaded into theft of customer data across connected platforms, including Salesforce, hitting security vendors Huntress and Recorded Future; affected organizations cannot reconnect Klue to Salesforce until further notice.

sources.title: Cybersecurity Firms Impacted by Klue Supply Chain Attack
Salesforce Disables Klue App Integration After OAuth Token Abuse Exposes Customer Data
Klue breach lead to Salesforce data theft, Huntress affected
Salesforce Data Thefts Continue via Klue App Compromise

topics.title: FortiBleed Campaign Hits 86,644 FortiGate Devices
topics.latest_developments: CISA, in a June 18 advisory, attributed the FortiBleed credential theft to Russian-speaking threat actors and put the tally at 86,644 compromised FortiGate appliances—roughly half the internet-accessible Fortinet firewalls and VPNs.
topics.summary: FortiBleed leaked login credentials for tens of thousands of internet-facing Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways; CISA urges customers to rotate credentials and harden devices.

sources.title: CISA Warns Fortinet Customers as FortiBleed Hits 86,644 FortiGate Devices
FortiBleed: 86,000 Fortinet Device Credentials Compromised
CISA warns Fortinet users to secure devices after FortiBleed leak

topics.title: AutoJack Turns AI Browsing Agents Into RCE
topics.latest_developments: Microsoft detailed AutoJack, an exploit chain in which one malicious webpage drives an AI browsing agent into remote code execution on the host by abusing localhost trust and AutoGen Studio's unauthenticated MCP WebSocket.
topics.summary: As enterprises grant AI agents broad system access, a single page or unmanaged agent identity can pivot to host compromise or data exfiltration; treat agents as privileged identities and lock down the local services they reach.

sources.title: AutoJack: How a single page can RCE the host running your AI agent
Every AI Agent Is an Identity. Most Organizations Don't Treat Them That Way

topics.title: Popa TV-Box Botnet Tied to Israel's Alarum
topics.latest_developments: Researchers linked the four-year-old Popa botnet—millions of hijacked Android TV boxes relaying traffic for ad fraud, account takeovers, and scraping—to NetNut, the residential-proxy arm of publicly traded Israeli firm Alarum Technologies.
topics.summary: Popa conscripts consumer TV boxes into a residential proxy network used for fraud and mass data scraping; its tie to a NASDAQ-listed proxy provider raises questions about the legitimacy of the residential-proxy industry.

sources.title: 'Popa' Botnet Linked to Publicly-Traded Israeli Firm
In Other News: Apple Patches Beats Eavesdropping Flaw, DOT Closes Delta CrowdStrike Probe, AWS Continuum

topics.title: F5 Patches Critical NGINX Open Source RCE Flaws
topics.latest_developments: F5 shipped fixes for two critical NGINX Open Source flaws, including CVE-2026-42530, a CVSS 9.2 use-after-free in the ngx_http_v3_module that a remote unauthenticated attacker can trigger for code execution.
topics.summary: NGINX Open Source powers a large share of the world's web servers; operators should apply F5's updates promptly to close the unauthenticated remote code execution path.

sources.title: F5 Patches Two Critical NGINX Open Source Flaws Enabling Remote Code Execution

topics.title: China-Linked UNC6508 Targets Outdated REDCap Servers
topics.latest_developments: SecurityWeek reported that most internet-accessible REDCap research-data servers run outdated software that China-linked UNC6508 regularly exploits for initial access and backdoor deployment.
topics.summary: REDCap is a widely used research data-collection platform across universities and hospitals; administrators should update internet-facing instances to block UNC6508 intrusions.

sources.title: Majority of Internet-Accessible REDCap Servers Outdated

weather: Juneteenth: Slight Chance Rain Showers, high 78F.
Saturday: Mostly Sunny then Slight Chance Showers And Thunderstorms, high 78F.

business_politics.title: Iran Talks Postponed, Israel and Hezbollah Renew Ceasefire
business_politics.latest_developments: Israel struck targets across southern Lebanon overnight and Iran fired warning shots in the Strait of Hormuz, prompting Tehran to push back the U.S.-Iran nuclear talks in Switzerland—Vice President JD Vance's fueled plane never left—before Israel and Hezbollah agreed Friday to renew their ceasefire and oil eased on hopes the wider accord survives.
business_politics.summary: The United States and Iran are negotiating a 60-day interim deal to end their war, reopen the Strait of Hormuz, and release $6 billion in Iranian funds frozen in Qatar; renewed fighting in Lebanon now threatens that timetable and keeps Gulf oil shipping on edge.

sources.title: How Fresh Middle East Violence Scuttled the Next Phase of Iran Talks
Israel, Hezbollah Agree to Renewed Ceasefire as Iran Peace Talks Postponed

business.title: Lautrec to Reopen at Nemacolin
business.latest_developments: Lautrec, the French fine-dining room at the Nemacolin resort in Farmington, Fayette County, reopens this July with a reimagined menu and an evening rebuilt around course-by-course theatrical spectacle.
business.summary: Nemacolin is relaunching its storied French restaurant Lautrec in July, reworking both the food and the choreography of its multi-course dinner service.

sources.title: Dining as Theater: Lautrec Reopens at Nemcolin

business.title: Former Heinz Estate Lists in Highland Park
business.latest_developments: A one-time Heinz-family estate in Pittsburgh's Highland Park neighborhood has come on the market, TribLive reports.
business.summary: A historic home tied to the Heinz family is now listed for sale in Highland Park, on Pittsburgh's east end.

sources.title: Former Heinz-owned estate for sale in Highland Park

around_town.title: Imagination Library Expands in Pittsburgh
around_town.latest_developments: The City of Pittsburgh and Reading Ready Pittsburgh announced Thursday they are expanding Dolly Parton's Imagination Library, unveiling the growth at the Phillips Recreation Center in Carrick.
around_town.summary: Dolly Parton's Imagination Library mails a free book each month to enrolled children from birth to age five; the expansion widens local enrollment through Reading Ready Pittsburgh.

sources.title: Dolly Parton's Imagination Library program to expand in Pittsburgh, boosting kids' access to books

around_town.title: PennDOT Readies Controlled Blast on Parkway East
around_town.latest_developments: With the Parkway East closure weeks away, PennDOT said the controlled blast that will demolish a bridge has been engineered so the detonation will not affect nearby homes.
around_town.summary: PennDOT plans to fully close the Parkway East and bring down a bridge by controlled blast, part of the bridge work that will also shut Commercial Street beneath the highway.

sources.title: Will nearby neighborhoods feel the impact of the controlled blast during Parkway construction?

events.title: Palace Theatre Marks Its Centennial
events.latest_developments: The century-old Palace Theatre in downtown Greensburg is celebrating 100 years with a time capsule and a LeAnn Rimes concert.
events.summary: Greensburg's Palace Theatre, in Westmoreland County, is staging a centennial celebration that includes a time capsule and a headlining performance by country singer LeAnn Rimes.

sources.title: Palace Theatre in Greensburg to celebrate 100 years with time capsule, Leann Rimes concert

events.title: Winona Fighter Plays the Roxian
events.latest_developments: Punk band Winona Fighter, fronted by Coco Kinnon, brings the idobi Radio Summer School tour to the Roxian Theatre in McKees Rocks.
events.summary: Winona Fighter, with new music in hand, headlines a stop on the idobi Radio Summer School tour at the Roxian Theatre in McKees Rocks.

sources.title: Q&A: Winona Fighter's Coco Kinnon talks new music, Summer School tour ahead of Roxian stop

around_teams.title: Steelers Expected to Pass on Sorsby
around_teams.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette's Gerry Dulac writes the Steelers are unlikely to bid on quarterback Brendan Sorsby in the NFL's supplemental draft, pointing to the gambling matter that pushed him there.
around_teams.summary: Brendan Sorsby, a college quarterback entering the league through the supplemental draft after a gambling issue, holds little appeal for a Steelers quarterback room coach Mike McCarthy is still sorting.

sources.title: Don't expect the Steelers to make a bid for Brendan Sorsby in the NFL's supplemental draft
WATCH: Why the Steelers would be right to avoid Brendan Sorsby

around_teams.title: Spears-Jennings Built His Case in 4.32 Seconds
around_teams.latest_developments: A Post-Gazette feature traces how Steelers rookie safety Robert Spears-Jennings reshaped his draft stock with a 4.32-second 40-yard dash at the NFL combine.
around_teams.summary: Robert Spears-Jennings, a rookie safety for the Steelers, turned a blazing 4.32-second 40 at the combine into a higher draft trajectory.

sources.title: How Steelers rookie safety Robert Spears-Jennings changed his NFL trajectory in 4.32 seconds

around_teams.title: SNR Drive Builds an All-Time Non-HOF Steelers Team
around_teams.latest_developments: On the June 17 SNR Drive, Matt Williamson and Wes Uhler assembled a team of the best Steelers who never reached the Hall of Fame and broke down running back Jaylen Warren's game.
around_teams.summary: The Steelers' SNR Drive podcast, hosted by Matt Williamson and Wes Uhler, picked top Steelers left out of Canton across offense and defense and analyzed current back Jaylen Warren.

sources.title: All-Time Non-HOF Team + Breaking Down Jaylen Warren | SNR Drive (June.17) | Pittsburgh Steelers

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: An Interview with Michael Morton About E-Commerce in the Age of AI
reading.summary: Ben Thompson interviews Michael Morton on how AI reshapes e-commerce, weighing unfalsifiable bear cases, distribution versus referral models, grocery, and autonomous vehicles.

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Exclusive: OpenAI Losses Increased Nearly 8X in 2025, With Spending Hitting $34 Billion
reading.summary: Zitron reports OpenAI's losses grew nearly eightfold in 2025 as spending reached $34 billion, arguing the company still has no path to profitability.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: AI Isn't Breaking Work. It's Already Broken.
reading.summary: Newport argues AI is exposing dysfunction already baked into knowledge work, riffing on a Financial Times interview with the Work AI Institute's Rebecca Hinds about a survey of 6,000 digital workers.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
